sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.40 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Does Thompson's group$F$(see 12.20) have the Howson property, that is, is the intersection of any two finitely generated subgroups of$F$finitely generated? { I.\,Kapovich} No, it does not. Otherwise any subgroup of$F$would have this property. But the wreath product$$, which does not have the Howson property (A.\,S.\,Kirkinski , Algebra Logic, { 20}, no.\,1 (1981), 24--36), can be embedded in$F$(S....

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
